titleEn: EU poultry producers spotlight AMR efforts during awareness week
titleZh: 欧盟家禽生产商在抗微生物药物耐药性意识周期间强调减抗努力
summaryZh: 本文报道了欧盟在2025年世界抗微生物药物耐药性意识周期间，通过宣传活动强调家禽生产中减少抗生素使用的严格措施。欧盟已禁止将抗生素用于促生长，仅在兽医监督下用于治疗，并配合生物安全、疫苗接种、健康监测等预防手段。文章还指出欧盟标准与韩国残留限量法规高度一致，确保家禽产品安全贸易。虽然内容涉及养殖业减抗这一重要议题，但未涉及智慧畜牧技术（如IoT、AI、自动化等），因此与智慧畜牧相关性较低。
featuredReason: 本文展示了欧盟在减少抗生素使用方面的严格法规和行业实践，对关注食品安全和可持续养殖的从业者具有参考价值，但缺乏智慧畜牧技术应用细节，适合作为行业背景了解。
contentFull: Markets & policy Market trends Sustainability EU poultry producers spotlight AMR efforts during awareness week Strict standards align with Korea’s rules to ensure safe poultry trade 18 November 2025 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media Asia To mark World Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) Awareness Week, which begins today (18–24 November), the EU co-financed the 'European Poultry – From Our Farms to Your Tables' information and promotional campaign to highlight the efforts of European poultry producers to reduce antimicrobial resistance (AMR), according to a recent press release. The European Union bans the use of antibiotics in animal production except when treatment is necessary due to illness. Treatment is carried out under strict veterinary supervision, observing the withdrawal period required to ensure that residues remain within legal limits. Prevention and welfare are a priority. In line with the Farm to Fork strategy, the EU aims to further reduce antimicrobial use while maintaining high food safety standards and full traceability.European poultry producers employ preventive measures such as strict on-farm biosecurity, vaccination programmes tailored to disease risk, daily flock-health monitoring, attention to water and feed quality, and comfortable housing conditions (ventilation, lighting, space). Medicines remain a treatment tool — used as rarely as possible and only when medically justified — to protect animal health and welfare. This approach promotes flock health, reduces the risk of antimicrobial resistance (AMR) and strengthens consumer trust.Thanks to these strict procedures, EU poultry meat is safe for consumers and does not contain harmful residues. This is ensured by EU and national laws that strictly control the use of veterinary medicines and the sale of meat.Antibiotic growth promoters in animal feed have been banned in South Korea since 1 July 2011. Their use is permitted only for therapeutic purposes and under strict veterinary supervision. The Positive List System (PLS) rule also applies: if no residue limit (MRL) is set, a very strict default threshold of 0.01 ppm (0.01 mg/kg) is enforced. These clear rules are designed to promote food safety and transparency in the supply chain.European production methods, based on clear principles of veterinary supervision, full traceability and risk assessment, are consistent with and meet the expectations of South Korean industry partners and consumers. The EU's stringent standards are at least equivalent to those of South Korea and reflect some of the highest requirements applied globally. Therefore, European poultry products fully comply with Korea's PLS and MRL requirements, ensuring a safe and predictable supply. Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all Australia ramps up avian influenza testing Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 24 June 2026 1 minute read H5N1 spreads to second Australian state Health & disease Diseases Avian influenza 24 June 2026 1 minute read Cases confirmed in South Australia after dead seabirds found Canada launches $3B food security strategy to boost domestic output Markets & policy Policy and regulation Food security 24 June 2026 1 minute read Plan targets grocery competition, local production and red tape cuts Our Partners World Poultry Science Association Congress Poultry Science Association Targan dsm-firmenich Lallemand Lanxess BioZyme Hy-Line International
translationZh:
为纪念今日（11月18日至24日）开始的世界抗微生物药物耐药性（AMR）意识周，欧盟共同资助了“欧洲家禽——从我们的农场到您的餐桌”信息与推广活动，以突出欧洲家禽生产商在减少抗微生物药物耐药性方面所做的努力，根据最近的一份新闻稿。

欧盟禁止在动物生产中使用抗生素，除非因疾病治疗需要。治疗必须在严格的兽医监督下进行，并遵守停药期，以确保残留物保持在法定限度内。预防和动物福利是优先事项。根据“从农场到餐桌”战略，欧盟旨在进一步减少抗菌药物的使用，同时维持高食品安全标准和完全可追溯性。

欧洲家禽生产商采取预防措施，如严格的农场生物安全、针对疾病风险定制的疫苗接种计划、每日鸡群健康监测、关注饮水和饲料质量，以及舒适的饲养环境（通风、光照、空间）。药物仍然是一种治疗工具——尽可能少用，且仅在医学上合理时使用——以保护动物健康和福利。这种方法促进了鸡群健康，降低了抗微生物药物耐药性（AMR）的风险，并增强了消费者信任。

得益于这些严格程序，欧盟禽肉对消费者是安全的，且不含有害残留物。欧盟及各国法律严格管控兽药的使用和肉类销售，从而确保了这一点。

自2011年7月1日起，韩国已禁止在动物饲料中使用抗生素生长促进剂。仅允许出于治疗目的并在严格兽医监督下使用。阳性列表系统（PLS）规则同样适用：如果未设定残留限量（MRL），则执行非常严格的默认阈值0.01 ppm（0.01 mg/kg）。这些明确规则旨在促进食品安全和供应链透明度。

基于兽医监督、完全可追溯性和风险评估等明确原则的欧洲生产方法，与韩国行业伙伴和消费者的期望相一致并满足其要求。欧盟的严格标准至少与韩国相当，并反映了全球范围内最高的一些要求。因此，欧洲家禽产品完全符合韩国的PLS和MRL要求，确保了安全且可预测的供应。
contentHtml:
EU poultry producers spotlight AMR efforts during awareness week 
Strict standards align with Korea’s rules to ensure safe poultry trade
